author: Antigravity Agent
funFacts: pufferfishs inflate their bodies into a spike-covered ball by swallowing large amounts of water to deter predators.
Most species of pufferfish contain tetrodotoxin, a substance up to 1,200 times more toxic than cyanide, located in their internal organs.
